zh.Nettlecombe, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特內特萊可姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Raleigh於大約1265年出生在英國薩默塞特內特萊可姆時，他的父親Simon de Raleigh43歲，母親Joan le Tort47歲。他於1320年與Margaret Le Bret結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1340年在英國英格蘭薩默塞特享年76歲。

zh-hans.Nettlecombe, Somerset, England: 英国薩默塞特內特萊可姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John de Raleigh于大约1265年出生在英国薩默塞特內特萊可姆时，他的父亲Simon de Raleigh43岁，母亲Joan le Tort47岁。他于1320年与Margaret Le Bret结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1340年在英国英格兰薩默塞特享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: Raleigh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Raleigh (House) in Pilton (Devon), recorded as Radeleia in 1086 and Rawlegh in 1535. The placename probably derives from Old English rǣge ‘roe deer’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

History: The English explorer Sir Walter Raleigh (1554–1618) was born in Hayes Barton, Devon, into a family of Devon gentry. He was related to most of the West Country's important families, including that of Sir Francis Drake. His half-brother was the explorer Sir Humphrey Gilbert. In 1578 Raleigh was granted a patent to explore and colonize “unknown lands” in America. In the 1580s he was granted extensive estates in Munster (Ireland), in reward for his suppression of the Desmond Rebellions.

variants.name: Rawleigh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Orlo.
English: variant of   Raleigh  , reflecting a common pronunciation.

variants.name: Riley
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name chiefly from High Ryley in Accrington (Lancashire) and Riley in Kirkburton, which was in the manor of Wakefield (Yorkshire). In some cases the name may derive from Riley Farm in Eyam (Derbyshire), Royley in Royton (Lancashire), or Rylah in Scarcliffe (Derbyshire). The placenames all come from Old English rȳge ‘rye’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

Irish: from Ó Raghailligh ‘descendant of Raghailleach’, Old Irish Roghallach. This is the name of a chieftain family in Cavan, related to the O'Rourkes. In Anglicized form it has also been confused with Ó Raithile found in Munster, usually Anglicized as O'Rahilly or Rahilly . See O'Reilly .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Raleigh was born about 1265, in Nettlecombe, Somerset, England, his father, Simon de Raleigh, was 43 and his mother, Joan le Tort, was 47. He married Margaret Le Bret in 1320. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1340, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Raleigh est né environ 1265, à Nettlecombe, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Simon de Raleigh, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Joan le Tort, avait 47 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Le Bret en 1320. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1340, à Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 76 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John de Raleigh um 1265 in Nettlecombe, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Simon de Raleigh 43 und seine Mutter Joan le Tort 47. Er hat 1320 Margaret Le Bret geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1340 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John de Raleigh è nato il circa 1265 nel Nettlecombe, Somerset, England, il suo padre, Simon de Raleigh, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Joan le Tort, aveva 47 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Le Bret nel 1320. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1340, nel Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 76 anni.

ja.Nettlecombe, Somerset, England: イギリスソメルセトネットレコンベ
ja.lifeSummary: John de Raleighが約1265年にイギリスソメルセトネットレコンベで生まれた時，父Simon de Raleighは43歳，母Joan le Tortは47歳であった。1320年にMargaret Le Bretと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1340年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトで76歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Raleigh(이)가 대략 1265년 영국 서머세트 네틀렉브에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Simon de Raleigh(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan le Tort(은)는 47세였다. 그는 1320년 Margaret Le Bret(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1340년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John de Raleigh nasceu aproximadamente 1265, em Nettlecombe, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Simon de Raleigh, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Joan le Tort, tinha 47 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Le Bret em 1320. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1340, em Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 76 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Raleigh родился около 1265 г. в местности неттлекомб, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Simon de Raleigh было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Joan le Tort, 47. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Le Bret в 1320 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1340 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 76 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John de Raleigh nació aproximadamente 1265, en Nettlecombe, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Simon de Raleigh, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Joan le Tort, tenía 47 años. Se casó con Margaret Le Bret en 1320. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1340, en Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 76 años.
